stories.title: Batch artifact write after analysis
stories.story: As a framework developer, I want to write all analysis artifacts in a single tool call so that post-analysis finalization completes in seconds instead of minutes.

stories.title: Crash-safe file writes
stories.story: As a Claude Code user, I want file writes to be atomic so that a session crash never leaves a half-written file on disk.

stories.title: Batch file read for implementation handoff
stories.story: As a framework developer, I want to read all test case files in a single tool call so that the implementation agent can start coding immediately after test design.

stories.title: Incremental section update during progressive writes
stories.story: As a framework developer, I want to update a specific section of a markdown artifact without rewriting the entire file so that progressive writes are faster and generate less I/O.

stories.title: Partial failure recovery
stories.story: As a Claude Code user, I want to know exactly which files succeeded and which failed in a batch operation so that I can retry only the failures.

stories.title: Directory creation for new backlog items
stories.story: As a framework developer, I want to create artifact directories in batch so that the add and analyze verbs can set up folder structures in a single call.

stories.title: Graceful fallback when server is unavailable
stories.story: As a Claude Code user, I want workflows to continue normally even if the bulk I/O MCP server is not installed so that the server is an optional performance enhancement, not a hard dependency.

stories.title: Standalone package for reuse
stories.story: As an MCP server consumer, I want the bulk file I/O server to be a standalone package with no framework-specific dependencies so that I can use it in any Claude Code project.
